questions.question: A bag contains 5 red marbles, 3 blue marbles, and 2 green marbles. You draw two marbles without replacement. What is the probability that you draw one red and one blue marble (in any order)? Express your answer as a fraction in lowest terms.
questions.explanation: Total ways to draw 2 marbles from 10: C(10,2) = 45. Ways to draw 1 red and 1 blue: 5 × 3 = 15. Probability = 15/45 = 5/12.

questions.question: In a class, 60% of students play soccer and 40% play basketball. Of those who play soccer, 25% also play basketball. If a student is chosen at random, what is the probability that the student plays basketball but not soccer?
questions.explanation: P(soccer and basketball) = 0.60 × 0.25 = 0.15. P(basketball) = 0.40. P(basketball but not soccer) = 0.40 − 0.15 = 0.25.

questions.question: A two-way table shows: 120 students take French, 80 take Spanish. Of the French students, 30 also take Spanish. Of the 200 total students surveyed, what is the probability a randomly selected student takes Spanish given that they take French?
questions.explanation: This is conditional probability: P(Spanish | French) = P(Spanish and French) / P(French) = 30/120 = 1/4.

questions.question: Two fair six-sided dice are rolled. What is the probability that the sum is greater than 9 OR both dice show an even number? Express as a fraction in lowest terms.
questions.explanation: Use inclusion–exclusion. Sum > 9: 6 outcomes. Both even: 9 outcomes. Both conditions (overlap): 3 outcomes. Total: 6 + 9 − 3 = 12 out of 36 = 1/3.

questions.question: A factory produces light bulbs. The probability that a bulb is defective is 0.05. If you randomly select 3 bulbs, what is the probability that at least one is defective? Round to the nearest hundredth.
questions.explanation: P(at least one defective) = 1 − P(none defective) = 1 − (0.95)³ = 1 − 0.857375 ≈ 0.14.

questions.question: A card is drawn from a standard 52-card deck. Given that the card is a face card (Jack, Queen, or King), what is the probability it is a heart? Express as a fraction.
questions.explanation: There are 12 face cards total (3 per suit × 4 suits). Of these, 3 are hearts. P(heart | face card) = 3/12 = 1/4.
